rulings.Case Title: IN RE: NATIONAL DEFAULT SERVICING CORPORATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION / Notice Of Motion & Motion To Allow Statement Of Claim To Surplus Funds By Administrator Of Decedent'S Estate Pursuant To Civil Code 2924ka4 And Distribution Of Funds To The Estate
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 20, 2019 line 1. CLAIMANT RENATA KIEFER MOTION / Notice Of Motion & Motion To Allow Statement Of Claim To Surplus Funds By Administrator Of Decedent'S Estate Pursuant To Civil Code 2924ka4 And Distribution Of Funds To The Estate GRANTED. The Court shall release the funds upon filing of a bond of cash plus the estimated annual income on the proceeds with the probate court. A status hearing will be set for July 26, 2019. If the Claimant is not bondable, the Court will distribute the funds to a blocked account. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN PAXTON VS. 330 PRESIDIO AVENUE, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Third Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 20, 2019 line 3. PLAINTIFF JOHN PAXTON Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Third Amended Complaint GRANTED. rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 20, 2019 line 4. DEFENDANT MELE AFUHAAMONGO MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND DENIED. Verification is sufficient and judgment is not void. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: SIMPLEX INVESTMENT HOLDINGS, LLC VS. MELECIO MAGDALUYO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Order To Show Cause Re: Contempt
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 20, 2019 line 5. Order To Show Cause Re: Contempt The Court finds that Mr. Estavillo failed to appear for trial call on May 13, 2019, after his May 10, 2019 ex-parte application to continue the trial had been denied, having chosen to proceed with his vacation plans starting on May 11, 2019. This conduct does not rise to the contempt level and the OSC re: contempt is discharged. Mr. Estavillo to present a copy of the order in conformity with this tentative ruling for execution at the hearing. The Clerk of the Court shall serve a copy of this Order and a copy of May 15, 2019 Order on the State Bar. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: PETER LU VS. JOSE A. D VASQUEZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave Of Court To Amend And Amended Complaint Pursuant To Ccp Sec 1166(D)(2); Memo Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Nicholas Goldman; Exhibits; Proposed Order
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 20, 2019 line 6. PLAINTIFF PETER LU Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave Of Court To Amend And Amended Complaint Pursuant To CCP Sec 1166(D)(2) OFF CALENDAR. No proof of service on file. Plaintiff was ordered to serve the moving papers on June 12, 2019. Proof of service must be filed 5 court days before the hearing, i.e. June 13, 2019. CRC 3.1300. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: CARTA HOLDINGS WASHINGTON LLC VS. YOUN UNG KANG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorney'S Fees
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 20, 2019 line 7. DEFENDANT YOUN KANG Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorney'S Fees Defendant's motion for attorney's fees is DENIED. Moving party failed to cite any cases standing for the proposition that fees may be recovered under the circumstances under SFRO 37.10B(c)(6) or CC 1942.5(i). While retaliatory eviction is an affirmative defense in an unlawful detainer action, Plaintiff failed to cite any authority for the proposition that CC 1942.5(i) is applicable to affirmative defenses or to point to any facts showing that Defendant is the prevailing party on the action/defense for retaliatory eviction. Parties additionally fail to discuss Larson v. CCSF (2011) 192 Cal.App.4th 1263. Plaintiff's request to strike or tax costs is DENIED. No motion to strike or tax costs is properly before the Court. To the extent, Planitff's opposition to the motion for attorney's fees may be construed as a motion to strike/tax costs, such motion is untimely per CCP 1005 and 1013. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: RONALD B. ROMINE VS. BLAIR ADAMS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Sanctions & Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 20, 2019 line 8. PLAINTIFF RONALD ROMINE Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Sanctions & Monetary Sanctions GRANTED and stayed until July 8, 2019 for Defendant to provide complete verified responses in accordance with June 5, 2019 by hand-delivery to Plaintiff's counsel by no later than 5:00 p.m. Sanctions granted in the amount of $900 to be paid within 15 days of notice of entry of order. This order to be served forthwith on Defendant's counsel. Defendant's counsel to immediately forward the order to the client. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: RONALD B. ROMINE VS. BLAIR ADAMS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 20, 2019 line 8. PLAINTIFF RONALD ROMINE Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant GRANTED. Defendant to provide complete verified responses without objections to the second set of discovery 2019 by hand-delivery to Plaintiff's counsel by no later than 5:00 p.m. Sanctions granted in the amount of $1,200 to be paid within 15 days of notice of entry of order. This order to be served forthwith on Defendant's counsel. Defendant's counsel to immediately forward the order to the client. = (501/CFH)
